title: perpetual calendar, reference 3940 was in manufactured - between 1985 and 2007.
brand: perpetual calendar, reference 3940 was in manufactured
overview: Often considered Patek Philippe’s quintessential automatic perpetual calendar, reference 3940 was in manufactured between 1985 and 2007. Exceptionally, this 3940 produced in 2015, is part of a limited edition of watches that Patek Philippe released and exhibited at the Saatchi Gallery in London to celebrate its 175th anniversary. For the occasion, Patek Philippe combined new old stock cases with dial colours never seen before in the references and even using different dial layouts, in this case that identical to the reference 5140. The fascination and mystery of this limited edition has led to much speculation among experts who estimate the production amounts to as few as 5 examples for each dial variation.  This is therefore an incredibly rare opportunity to acquire a very special edition of a very special, and historically important,

overview: off-centered dial configuration and the novel outsize date give freshness to the traditional features of the German timepieces such as the three-quarter plate and the screwed gold chatons. While its display is seemingly disordered, the placement of the dial is the result of precise calculations and complex rules of symmetry, relying on the mathematics of the golden ratio and perfectly balanced. But when it comes to watchmaking, A. Lange & Söhne is not only about appearance, and the present reference is a wonderful example of the intricacies and great care given to the process of making the movement. With its characteristic bridges in German silver - a nickel, copper and zinc alloy - the Lange 1 is powered by the manual-winding calibre  901.0 with 53 jewels and more than 350 components which run A. Lange & Söhne’s signature digital date display window and a power

title: announcement of their first collections, the Datograph is - named after the complications “date” and “chronograph”. It
brand: announcement of their first collections, the Datograph is
model: named after the complications “date” and “chronograph”. It
overview: Introduced in 1999, five years after A. Lange & Söhne’s announcement of their first collections, the Datograph is named after the complications “date” and “chronograph”. It is the exemplifier of German watchmaking and best known for their finishing, complexity and iconic design. Its calibre L951 features a flyback chronograph with a jumping minute counter, the beautiful column wheel mechanism meticulously displayed through the sapphire display back. In fact, master watchmaker Philippe Dufour owns the pink gold version of the Datograph and describes it as the best chronograph movement ever made. Basketball player Michael Jordan also wears the first generation platinum Datograph with a matching bracelet. From the horology world to celebrities, the Datograph is widely enjoyed by different collectors and showcases the splendid capabilities of German watchmaking.

title: be mistaken for having a rubellite dial.  However, the material - used here is known as ‘Coeur de Rubis’ (Heart of Ruby), which
brand: be mistaken for having a rubellite dial.  However, the material
model: used here is known as ‘Coeur de Rubis’ (Heart of Ruby), which
overview: Distinguished by its hardstone dial, this reference 16523, might be mistaken for having a rubellite dial.  However, the material used here is known as ‘Coeur de Rubis’ (Heart of Ruby), which differs somewhat in appearance and colour providing a more uniform and much deeper red.  The stone is incredibly fragile and become even more so as it is worked thinner and thinner by expert craftsmen.  It is, as a result, a material seldom used and those that dare usually make only very few. Each piece is ultimately a unique piece. Originally, this timepiece was a special order made for Mr. Tarek Shah, who was at the time one of Rolex’s precious and ornamental stones supplier. He made this special request and supplied the stone that Rolex would use to make his dial. To the best of our knowledge this unusual ‘Zenith’ Daytona appears fresh to the market and represents the first time a

title: manual, numbered hang tag and presentation case with outer - packaging
brand: manual, numbered hang tag and presentation case with outer
overview: Accessories: Rolex International guarantee card, instruction manual, numbered hang tag and presentation case with outer packaging Over the deacdes, the Rolex Daytona has developed itself to become one of the most reliable and desirable chronographs on the market. Following the introduction of its in-house calibre in 2000, Rolex seeked several ways to make this model standout. They began this production with the ‘Beach’ Daytona, with their stone and mother-of-pearl dial, and rapidly developped new materials to further grow their catalog. This chronograph is, in some ways, an expression of Rolex’s know-how. It is equipped with a Cerachrome bezel, a technologically advanced ceramic bezel which will withstand the trial of time, an Oysterflex bracelet and a supperbe mother- of-pearl and diamond-set dial.

title: electricity was not granted to the general public, Patek Philippe - was determined to discover a system that can convert light
brand: electricity was not granted to the general public, Patek Philippe
model: was determined to discover a system that can convert light
overview: and nuclear timekeeping since 1948. At the time when stable electricity was not granted to the general public, Patek Philippe was determined to discover a system that can convert light into potential energy for powering horological devices. This had led to the ground-breaking invention of the manual caliber 17-250, a movement which harnesses solar panels and accumulators to power a mechanical clock like the Dome clock. While Dome clocks are often not as well-known as some of Patek Philippe’s wristwatches, they are one of the most collectible products of Patek Philippe. Characterized by their cylindrical form, spherical top and often found finely decorated with traditional artisanal techniques such as cloisonné enamels, each clock is unique by its own ornamentations. Rendered in polychrome enamel which finely recreates a

overview: The Ellipsocurvex was released at the wake of the 21st century in a limited edition of 210 pieces. Its tonneau-shaped white gold case displays an innovative jumping-hour layout with a retrograde hour indication. The running seconds fill the centre of the display, in a dedicated window, creating a striking multi- layer effect. The timepiece is powered by the caliber DR115, a skillfully optimized 27 jewel automatic movement. This rare limited edition timepiece will certainly appeal to those collectors who appreciate innovative and creative time display. †  CHF 15,000-30,000 63 64 DANIEL ROTH ACADÉMIE ELLIPSOCURVEX PAPILLON, REFERENCE 318.Y.60 A LIMITED EDITION WHITE GOLD AND DIAMOND-SET

title: the Chronometre a Resonance.  It is arguably Journe’s most - iconic and most recognisable wristwatch, embodying all of its
brand: the Chronometre a Resonance.  It is arguably Journe’s most
model: iconic and most recognisable wristwatch, embodying all of its
overview: When many think of FP Journe they immediately think of the Chronometre a Resonance.  It is arguably Journe’s most iconic and most recognisable wristwatch, embodying all of its maker’s genius both technically and aesthetically. As a timepiece it embodies many of the fundamental elements that make the world of horology so alluring a place. It has history, a story behind how and why it was created; it has an unparalleled  aesthetics inspired by some of world’s best watchmakers though, at the same time, original and true to Journe’s personal vision; and within, working largely unseen, is the movement. A mechanical marvel again founded in horological history and bought to life by Journe’s incomparable skill, style and finesse. Resonance, the phenomenon, in short refers to when components placed within close proximity and enclosed in the

title: sophisticated model which follows the tradition of F.P. Journe’s - Octa collection. In the year 2001, F.P. Journe invented the
brand: sophisticated model which follows the tradition of F.P. Journe’s
model: Octa collection. In the year 2001, F.P. Journe invented the
overview: Produced between 2003 and 2021 the Octa Divine is a sophisticated model which follows the tradition of F.P. Journe’s Octa collection. In the year 2001, F.P. Journe invented the calibre 1300 with the vision of enhancing the power reserve and stability of a regular automatic movement. The result is a powerful movement with a power reserve of 120 hours, along with astounding versatility for complications to be interpreted. Based on the calibre 1300, collections such as the Octa UTC, Octa Lune, and of course, the present Octa Divine were born. The present Octa Divine is powered by the calibre 1300.3, which is forged in pink gold and features a uni-directional, 22k gold rotor for exceptional efficiency. The innovative aesthetics features the hours and minutes circle screwed to the dial, on the other hand, the off-centered subsidiary seconds and elegant moon phases balance each side out.

title: and created the Royal Oak, which went on to change the - way stainless steel was perceived in watchmaking. The
brand: and created the Royal Oak, which went on to change the
model: way stainless steel was perceived in watchmaking. The
overview: In 1972, acclaimed watch designer Gerald Genta designed and created the Royal Oak, which went on to change the way stainless steel was perceived in watchmaking. The preciousness and high price tag of the original Royal Oak shifted the tool watch in stainless steel to the ranks of precious metals, showcasing exceptional handwork and finishing. The Royal Oak eventually helped build a legacy which is stronger than ever 47 years later, paving the way for most luxury stainless steel timepieces offered on the market today. The present yellow gold Royal Oak delivers the day of the week and date displays, coupled with a moon-phase indicator at 6 o’clock. The 36mm case size allows for a dressier wear and makes it suitable for all wrist sizes. †  CHF 70,000-90,000 72

overview: The present lot is distinguished by the ‘Foundation’ designation to the dial. One of the most desirable models on the market today, it is very exciting to discover a Royal Oak that sets itself apart from the rest in such a way. Withstanding the challenge of time, the Royal Oak is an iconic model that has helped Audemars Piguet through the devasting period of the ‘Quartz Crisis’. The present watch features its iconic octagonal-shaped bezel secured by eight visible hexagonal screws, inspired by a traditional diver’s helmet. †  CHF 24,000-50,000 74 75 AUDEMARS PIGUET ROYAL OAK ‘FOUNDATION’, REFERENCE 14990ST. title: 79 - AUDEMARS PIGUET
overview: 78 79 AUDEMARS PIGUET AUDEMARS PIGUET ROYAL OAK The present lot, the Audemars Piguet annual calendar reference 25920, has quite the story. The watch itself represents the only annual calendar watch the manufacture has ever produced and it only enjoyed a very brief production, being introduced in 1999 and ending in the early 2000s.  This example from 2003 was gifted to the present owner, a sail maker, for renowned sailing team Alinghi,  for his participation and the team’s subsequent victory in the 31st Americas Cup. Team Alinghi, founded in 2000, was created solely to compete in the 2003 America’s Cup, the oldest trophy in international sport. In order to challenge the America’s Cup however they

title: of the Omega Speedmaster, one being the 145.022, a - reference which featured the lyre lugs and asymmetric case. In
brand: of the Omega Speedmaster, one being the 145.022, a
model: reference which featured the lyre lugs and asymmetric case. In
overview: There are several references which have become archetypes of the Omega Speedmaster, one being the 145.022, a reference which featured the lyre lugs and asymmetric case. In one watch, you can see very little changes from the late 1960s through today’s production. The present lot stands apart from many other example as it features a misprinted bezel indication 220 in the place of 200. This was a mistake very quickly spotted and corrected by Omega and as such watches displaying this error are exceptionally rare. The Omega Speedmaster Ref. 145.022 was the first Speedmaster housing the new caliber 861 movement that was introduced in 1968 as the successor of the caliber 321. The new movement ticked at 21,600vph and thus was more accurate than the caliber 321 that ticked at 18,000vph. CHF 10,000-15,000
